chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So next we are going to talk about JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: whose full form is JavaScript Extension Syntax

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So this is a syntax whose function is to combine JavaScript with HTML

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So it lets us write HTML directly inside JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Now in this, this JSX syntax cannot be directly converted into JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It's not like you can write JSX in a JavaScript file in a normal project

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and our code will run

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So JSX is not real JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It's not the real JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: The syntax we have learnt a lot about JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: all that syntax is not followed here

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: But what JSX does is, whenever code is written in JSX form in React

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: it is internally converted to JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: That code is transpiled

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: And that code has a special tool which transpiles it, which is called Babel

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Babel is a tool which transpiles JSX code

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It converts it to pure JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So whenever we write JSX code in React

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: First that code is in JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: After that Babel is used as an internal tool

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which converts it to JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: And finally, the rendering on the page, or the logic of our page

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: or whatever things are displayed on our browser

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: all that is displayed with the help of this JavaScript code

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Now as a beginner, the first question that can hit our mind is

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: that when we had to display JavaScript on the browser

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Why did we write code in JSX?

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Babel is transpiling it separately

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and converting it separately, and then converting it to JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: The simplest answer to this is that

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: if we learn JSX once

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: like we will cover JSX in a couple of classes

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So it is very easy to learn JSX and implement it

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: When we start writing things component-wise in JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: it becomes more readable

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and it becomes easier to write as compared to normal JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: to render the same amount of UI components

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: But this entire conversion

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: the entire conversion from JSX to JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: the entire conversion that Babel does internally

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: is done behind the scenes

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We will never see this happening

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It is done internally by itself

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: When we set up React in our computer

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: then our internal tools will automatically do this conversion

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We will not have to write any code separately

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We will not have to do anything separately

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: If we want, we can also go to the Babel page

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which is babeljs.io

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: If we go here

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: If we click on try it out

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: then some code like this will open for us

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Now in the sandbox

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which we saw earlier as the code for the app

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Let's copy this

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and let's paste it here

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So this was the code for React

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which we wrote in JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So Babel internally changes it in this format

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: And this format is our pure JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: In this, you will see all the JavaScript things

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Function, MyButton

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: What is returning?

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Some call is being done with the name JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: in which a button is being passed as a string

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: After that, an object is being passed

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: in which it is written in children

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: After that, we have another function

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: with the name MyApp

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It is also returning something by saying JSXS

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: in which a div is being created

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: An H1 element is being created in the children of the div

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So this is how Babel works

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So Babel basically converts our pure JSX code

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: into normal JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: But this conversion will be done automatically by Babel

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We don't need to partake in this

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So this was the basics of how React works

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and in which format the code is written

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Now we are finally going to set up React

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: on our local machine

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We will start creating a React project

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: a basic app

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: And with that, we will slowly understand

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: how we can build our components

text: So next we are going to talk about JSX whose full form is JavaScript Extension Syntax So this is a syntax whose function is to combine JavaScript with HTML So it lets us write HTML directly inside JavaScript Now in this, this JSX syntax cannot be directly converted into JavaScript It's not like you can write JSX in a JavaScript file in a normal project and our code will run So JSX is not real JavaScript It's not the real JavaScript The syntax we have learnt a lot about JavaScript all that syntax is not followed here But what JSX does is, whenever code is written in JSX form in React it is internally converted to JavaScript That code is transpiled And that code has a special tool which transpiles it, which is called Babel Babel is a tool which transpiles JSX code It converts it to pure JavaScript So whenever we write JSX code in React First that code is in JSX After that Babel is used as an internal tool which converts it to JavaScript And finally, the rendering on the page, or the logic of our page or whatever things are displayed on our browser all that is displayed with the help of this JavaScript code Now as a beginner, the first question that can hit our mind is that when we had to display JavaScript on the browser Why did we write code in JSX? Babel is transpiling it separately and converting it separately, and then converting it to JavaScript The simplest answer to this is that if we learn JSX once like we will cover JSX in a couple of classes So it is very easy to learn JSX and implement it When we start writing things component-wise in JSX it becomes more readable and it becomes easier to write as compared to normal JavaScript to render the same amount of UI components But this entire conversion the entire conversion from JSX to JavaScript the entire conversion that Babel does internally is done behind the scenes We will never see this happening It is done internally by itself When we set up React in our computer then our internal tools will automatically do this conversion We will not have to write any code separately We will not have to do anything separately If we want, we can also go to the Babel page which is babeljs.io If we go here If we click on try it out then some code like this will open for us Now in the sandbox which we saw earlier as the code for the app Let's copy this and let's paste it here So this was the code for React which we wrote in JSX So Babel internally changes it in this format And this format is our pure JavaScript In this, you will see all the JavaScript things Function, MyButton What is returning? Some call is being done with the name JSX in which a button is being passed as a string After that, an object is being passed in which it is written in children After that, we have another function with the name MyApp It is also returning something by saying JSXS in which a div is being created An H1 element is being created in the children of the div So this is how Babel works So Babel basically converts our pure JSX code into normal JavaScript But this conversion will be done automatically by Babel We don't need to partake in this So this was the basics of how React works and in which format the code is written Now we are finally going to set up React on our local machine We will start creating a React project a basic app And with that, we will slowly understand how we can build our components
